gog-and-magog.script: Gog and Magog. As you saw earlier, when Dhul-Qarnain raised his great wall, the Holy Quran issued a warning. It reads, in chapter eighteen, verses ninety-nine and one hundred: "'This is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord shall come to pass, He will break it into pieces. And the promise of my Lord is certainly true.' And on that day We shall leave some of them to surge against others, and the trumpet will be blown. Then shall We gather them all together." As we have seen, Gog and Magog were violent tribes invading from Europe and Russia into the lands of the Persians and Arabs. This verse prophesied that the walls would not hold forever; and so it was to be. In eighteen hundred and one, Russia took the land of the Darial Gorge. In eighteen thirteen, the Derbent fortifications fell to the Russians too. They would use these entry points to attack Iran for over a hundred years. The warning was fulfilled. But who are Gog and Magog, in truth? Here the Promised Messiah, Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad, unveils them as masters of fire. In his own words: "Since Ajij, from which the words Gog and Magog are derived, means 'fire', God Almighty has disclosed to me that Gog and Magog are a people who are greater experts in the use of fire than any other people. Their very names indicate that their ships, trains and machines will be run by fire. They will fight their battles with fire. They will excel all other people in harnessing fire to their service. This is why they will be called Gog and Magog. These are the people of the West, as they are unique in their expertise in the use of fire." And there is a spiritual dimension too, which he called the Dajjal. The greatest trial foretold of these Latter Days was that of the Dajjal, the Antichrist civilisation overspreading the world. Gog and Magog referred to their material aspects, while Dajjal referred to their spiritual aspects. As Hazrat Ahmad explained: "Dajjal in fact is none other than the people known as Christian missionaries and European philosophers. They act like the two jaws of the Promised Dajjal with which he devours people's faith like a python. First it is the common and ignorant people who get caught in the wiles of the missionaries; and then, those who happen to escape their clutches being disgusted with the disgraceful and false beliefs, are caught in the net of the European philosophers. I see that the common people are more vulnerable to the lies of the clergy, whereas the intellectual ones are more susceptible to the falsehood spread by the philosophers."

verses.text: 'This is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord shall come to pass, He will break it into pieces. And the promise of my Lord is certainly true.' And on that day We shall leave some of them to surge against others, and the trumpet will be blown. Then shall We gather them all together.

Since Ajij, from which the words Gog and Magog are derived, means 'fire', God Almighty has disclosed to me that Gog and Magog are a people who are greater experts in the use of fire than any other people. Their very names indicate that their ships, trains and machines will be run by fire. They will fight their battles with fire. They will excel all other people in harnessing fire to their service. This is why they will be called Gog and Magog. These are the people of the West, as they are unique in their expertise in the use of fire.

Dajjal in fact is none other than the people known as Christian missionaries and European philosophers. They act like the two jaws of the Promised Dajjal with which he devours people's faith like a python. First it is the common and ignorant people who get caught in the wiles of the missionaries; and then, those who happen to escape their clutches being disgusted with the disgraceful and false beliefs, are caught in the net of the European philosophers. I see that the common people are more vulnerable to the lies of the clergy, whereas the intellectual ones are more susceptible to the falsehood spread by the philosophers.

dhul-qarnain-revealed.script: Dhul-Qarnain. Is this a story of the past, or of the future? The Quran is full of secrets. One secret revealed by the Promised Messiah is this: that historical events narrated by the Quran foretell future events. And in the same way that the story of Dhul-Qarnain referred to the great Persian Emperor of the past, it also referred to the Promised Persian Messiah of the future. We will now examine how the verses of the Dhul-Qarnain story apply to Hazrat Ahmad, peace be upon him. Consider first the very meaning of the name. In his own words: "I have come as Dhul-Qarnain for this Ummah. It is evident that Dhul-Qarnain has to be a person who witnesses two centuries. And, it is a marvellous matter about me that when you look at all the calendars in use, you will find that I have lived in two centuries according to the calendars of all people." Consider, too, how he was established in the earth. He wrote: "I have been provided with all the means for the propagation of the truth which were not even available at the time of any other prophet. A piece of writing can now be published throughout the world within forty days, whereas previously a man could not achieve this even in a hundred years." Now recall the two great journeys of Dhul-Qarnain. First, the westward journey, to the setting of the sun. As the Promised Messiah explained: "These verses contain a prophecy that the Promised Messiah will appear at a time when the people of the West will be steeped in darkness. The Sun of Truth will completely disappear from their view, going down into a dirty, stinking pool; in other words, instead of truth, foul beliefs and practices will be prevalent among them. This would be their water which they will drink. They will have no light whatsoever and will wallow in darkness. And as is clear, this is exactly the condition of the Christian faith today as described by the Holy Quran, and the great centre of Christianity is also in the Western countries." And then the eastward journey, to the rising of the sun. He explained: "That is, Dhul-Qarnain will observe the state of the people of the East, and will discover a people at the place of the rising of the Sun of Truth who will be so ignorant that they will have no means of protecting themselves from the glare of the sun. That is, they will be scorched by their worship of external form and extremism, and they will be unaware of the truth. In this way, the rising Sun of Truth will bring about their destruction."

verses.text: I have come as Dhul-Qarnain for this Ummah... It is evident that Dhul-Qarnain has to be a person who witnesses two centuries. And, it is a marvellous matter about me that when you look at all the calendars in use, you will find that I have lived in two centuries according to the calendars of all people.

I have been provided with all the means for the propagation of the truth which were not even available at the time of any other prophet... A piece of writing can now be published throughout the world within forty days, whereas previously a man could not achieve this even in a hundred years.

...these verses contain a prophecy that the Promised Messiah will appear at a time when the people of the West will be steeped in darkness. The Sun of Truth will completely disappear from their view—going down into a dirty, stinking pool; in other words, instead of truth, foul beliefs and practices will be prevalent among them. This would be their water which they will drink. They will have no light whatsoever and will wallow in darkness. And as is clear, this is exactly the condition of the Christian faith today as described by the Holy Quran, and the great centre of Christianity is also in the Western countries.

That is, Dhul-Qarnain... will observe the state of the people of the East, and will discover a people at the place of the rising of the Sun of Truth who will be so ignorant that they will have no means of protecting themselves from the glare of the sun. That is, they will be scorched by their worship of external form and extremism, and they will be unaware of the truth... In this way, the rising Sun of Truth will bring about their destruction.

the-wall-of-islam.script: The Wall of Islam. The Promised Messiah described how Dhul-Qarnain would reach a third and critical way, a place between two barriers. In his words: "Dhul-Qarnain, the Promised Messiah, would then follow another course and would find himself at a very critical situation that can be described as being between two barriers or mountains. This means that he would encounter a time when people on either side would be in fear, and the powers of darkness, in collaboration with the powers of state, would present an awe-striking spectacle. Under both these powers he would discover a people who would find it difficult to understand him. They would be the victims of false beliefs on account of which they would find it difficult to understand the guidance which he would present to them, but in the end they would understand him and would attain guidance. They are the third kind of people who would benefit from the guidance of the Promised Messiah." To this people he gave a charge, to build a wall of iron. Recall from the Quran the words, 'Bring me slabs of iron.' The Promised Messiah explained their inner meaning: "That is, he would put forth such conclusive proofs and arguments that their enemies would not be left with any basis for criticism or objection against their religion. 'Bring me slabs of iron so that their movement can be stopped'; that is, hold fast to my teachings and my arguments, fully adopt steadfastness, and build yourselves like unto a wall of iron to repel the enemy's onslaught. 'Then blow fire into the iron until it becomes like fire'; that is, feed the flames of love for God until you yourselves assume the complexion of the Divine." And what would this community become? He said: "That is, the God of the Heavens will raise the Promised Messiah and create a third people for whom He will show great Signs of His support until all rightly inclined people are gathered upon one faith; that is to say, upon Islam. They will hear the voice of the Messiah and will hasten towards him; then will there be only one Shepherd and one flock." So the parallel is complete. What Cyrus the Great fulfilled in stone and conquest, Hazrat Ahmad fulfilled in spirit. Cyrus unified two kingdoms into one; expanded early eastward into the Turkish lands of muddy springs; expanded later westward into Central Asia; and built fortifications against the invading Scythians. The Promised Messiah joined two centuries in each calendar; brought the spiritual light of Islam to western peoples devoid of spirituality; fought against the extremism and literalism of eastern peoples; and founded a Community to act as a barrier against the Dajjal.

verses.text: Dhul-Qarnain—the Promised Messiah—would then follow another course and would find himself at a very critical situation that can be described as being between two barriers or mountains. This means that he would encounter a time when people on either side would be in fear, and the powers of darkness, in collaboration with the powers of state, would present an awe-striking spectacle. Under both these powers he would discover a people who would find it difficult to understand him. They would be the victims of false beliefs on account of which they would find it difficult to understand the guidance which he would present to them, but in the end they would understand him and would attain guidance. They are the third kind of people who would benefit from the guidance of the Promised Messiah.

That is, he would put forth such conclusive proofs and arguments that their enemies would not be left with any basis for criticism or objection against their religion. 'Bring me slabs of iron so that their movement can be stopped'; that is, hold fast to my teachings and my arguments, fully adopt steadfastness, and build yourselves like unto a wall of iron to repel the enemy's onslaught. 'Then blow fire into the iron until it becomes like fire'; that is, feed the flames of love for God until you yourselves assume the complexion of the Divine.

That is, the God of the Heavens will raise the Promised Messiah and create a third people for whom He will show great Signs of His support until all rightly inclined people are gathered upon one faith; that is to say, upon Islam. They will hear the voice of the Messiah and will hasten towards him; then will there be only one Shepherd and one flock.

the-palace-prophecy.script: The Palace Prophecy. On the fifteenth of January, nineteen hundred and six, the Promised Messiah received a striking revelation, later published in Badr on the nineteenth of January that same year: "The Palace of Chosroes has been shaken." This momentous prophecy concerned the modern rulers of Iran, the Qajar Monarchy. Until this revelation, the Qajar monarchy had enjoyed over a century of absolute imperial rule. The Shah had the full military and diplomatic support of Imperial Russia. Muzaffarud-Din Shah, the Shah at the time, had recently accepted some constitutional reforms, and this revelation indicated that a fundamental change in Iranian history was about to take place. Then came the coup. After the Shah's death in nineteen hundred and seven, conflict intensified between his successor, Mohammad Ali Shah, and the Iranian Parliament. In nineteen hundred and eight, the new Shah suspended the constitutional order and had Cossack forces shell the parliament building in Tehran. His crackdown briefly restored royal rule. However, pro-constitutional forces organised armed resistance. In nineteen hundred and nine, they captured Tehran and forced the Shah to abdicate in favour of his young son. The monarchy limped on before ending in nineteen twenty-five. The palace of Chosroes had truly been shaken. Alongside this stood a revelation about the uniting of Muslims. Received on the twentieth of November, nineteen hundred and five, it showed the unifying purpose of his advent: "Gather together all the Muslims on the face of the earth so that they should unite in one faith." And in a Persian revelation of the eleventh of March, nineteen hundred and six, he was told: "When the reign of the Messiah, the monarch begins, the Muslims who were Muslims in name only will be reconverted to Islam." He explained its meaning: "In this revelation, by the reign of the just monarch is meant the period of the call of this humble one. No worldly kingdom is indicated here, but the kingdom of heaven that has been bestowed upon me. The meaning of this revelation is that when the kingdom of heaven, which in the estimation of Allah is the period of the Promised Messiah, the effect of it was that those who were only Muslims in name began to be Muslims in fact, as has already happened in the case of about four hundred thousand of them."

verses.text: The Palace of Chosroes has been shaken.
Gather together all the Muslims on the face of the earth so that they should unite in one faith.
When the reign of the Messiah, the monarch begins, the Muslims who were Muslims in name only will be reconverted to Islam.

In this revelation, by the reign of the just monarch is meant the period of the call of this humble one. No worldly kingdom is indicated here, but the kingdom of heaven that has been bestowed upon me... the meaning of this revelation is that when the kingdom of heaven, which in the estimation of Allah is the period of the Promised Messiah... the effect of it was that those who were only Muslims in name began to be Muslims in fact, as has already happened in the case of about 400,000 of them.

a-blessed-dream.script: A Blessed Dream. We close our journey with a blessed dream of Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad, the Promised Messiah, peace be upon him. He relates: "I saw in a blessed dream a group of sincere believers and just and righteous kings, some of whom belonged to this country, some to Arabia, some to Iran, some to Syria, some to Turkey, and some to other regions of which I am not aware." Thereafter, he says, he was told by Allah the Almighty: "These people will affirm your righteousness and will believe in you and will call down blessings upon you and will pray for you. I shall bestow great blessings upon you, so much so that kings will seek blessings from your garments and I will join them amongst your sincere followers." And so our path comes to its end, having travelled from Cyrus of Persia to the Promised Messiah of Persian origin.

verses.text: I saw in a blessed dream a group of sincere believers and just and righteous kings, some of whom belonged to this country, some to Arabia, some to Iran, some to Syria, some to Turkey, and some to other regions of which I am not aware. Thereafter, I was told by Allah the Almighty:

verses.source: Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad (words of Allah the Almighty)
verses.text: These people will affirm your righteousness and will believe in you and will call down blessings upon you and will pray for you. I shall bestow great blessings upon you, so much so that kings will seek blessings from your garments and I will join them amongst your sincere followers.
